---
title: "Generative Structures"
---

This lesson treats an object as something that can be regenerated from a seed, rules, a schedule, and a closure condition.

The ordinary baseline is value-only storage:

```text
list every node
list every orbit element
list every diagram field
list every physics-loop edge
```

The Circle Calculus version records the construction:

```text
seed + rule set + schedule + closure condition
  -> generated object
  -> theorem ids
  -> dictionary ids
  -> regeneration check
```

::: {.warning-box}
A generator record is not automatically smaller, optimal, or more meaningful. Its Python fixtures are executable references, not proof artifacts. It is useful only when exact regeneration, reader clarity, or proof linkage improves.
:::

::: {.learning-unit}

## What You Should Learn

By the end of the page, you should be able to explain why `C_128` can be clearer as a seed-rule record while `C_1` can be clearer as an explicit object.

:::

## Ordinary Baseline

Explicit storage is straightforward. For a finite circle, store all nodes. For a coil, store the entire orbit. For a proof glyph, store the final displayed metadata. That baseline is important because the generator has to beat or explain something real.

## Circle Calculus Framing

Coils already have a generative shape:

```text
start node
  -> stride rule
  -> repeated rotation
  -> closure
```

Seed-rule provenance makes the construction explicit. The generated object still exists, but it is paired with the record that rebuilt it and the theorem/dictionary ids that explain what the record means.

## Executable Fixture

The Python reference model under `circle_math/generative.py` provides:

```text
SeedRuleProvenance
finite_circle_generator
finite_circle_diagram_generator
physics_loop_diagram_generator
coil_orbit_generator
orbit_decomposition_generator
proof_glyph_generator
regenerate
compare_generator_to_explicit
bounded_generator_search
```

The sidecar tests regenerate finite circle nodes, a finite-circle diagram with successor edges, a finite physics-loop diagram with normalized plaquette phases and holonomy, a stride orbit, the full stride-orbit decomposition, and proof-glyph metadata. The comparison fixture checks exact regeneration before comparing description length. The bounded search fixture ranks only an explicitly supplied finite candidate list; it does not claim global minimality. The widget below reads `site/data/generated/generator_index.json`, which is exported from the Python seed-rule fixtures, and exposes finite-circle, finite physics-loop, coil-orbit, orbit-family, and proof-glyph records as generated explanation with theorem-status badges pulled from the manifest.

<div class="widget-panel" data-widget="seed_rule_diagram_generator"></div>

The next widget focuses on the compression reframing directly. It compares explicit object storage with a seed-plus-rule record, includes a broken non-exact candidate, and reports bounded-search scope. It is a finite fixture, not an optimal-compression theorem.

<div class="widget-panel" data-widget="generator_comparison_search" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0005" data-dictionary-ids="COMMON-0064 COMMON-0065 COMMON-0066"></div>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/generative/seed_rule_diagram_generator.js"></script>
<script type="module" src="../../widgets/generative/generator_comparison_search.js"></script>

::: {.checkpoint-box}

Why should exact regeneration be checked before reporting that a generator is shorter than an explicit object?

:::

## Positive And Negative Cases

For a large finite circle, storing the seed `n` plus the rule "generate nodes `0` through `n-1`" is shorter than listing every node. For a one-node circle, the provenance metadata is longer than the object itself.

Both results are useful. The positive case shows constructive compression. The negative case prevents overclaiming.

## Proof Boundary

The theorem cards below are checked by Lean for finite regeneration statements. `GEN-T0003` proves exact regeneration of the representative-indexed orbit-schedule list. `GEN-T0006` through `GEN-T0013` add count, period, coverage, formal orbit-class agreement, canonical representative coverage, and disjointness facts. `GEN-T0014` through `GEN-T0019` make exact regeneration reusable as a reflexive, symmetric, transitive, and field-equality-gated relation for constructed generator comparisons. The bounded generator search is Python-only and finite-list scoped. These artifacts still do not prove optimality or minimality.

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0002"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0003"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0004"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0005"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0006"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0007"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0008"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0009"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0010"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0011"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0012"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0013"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0014"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0015"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0016"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0017"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0018"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0019"></div>

## Dictionary Trail

<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0064"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0065"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0066"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0033"></div>

## Source Trail

<p data-paper-ref="papers/generative/PAPER_GEN_01_SEED_RULE_PROVENANCE.md">Generative paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/generative/PAPER_GEN_01_SEED_RULE_PROVENANCE.md">Seed-Rule Provenance</a></p>
<p data-paper-ref="papers/S1/PAPER_S1_04_FACTORS_SCALING_PRIME_COILS.md">S1 source paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/S1/PAPER_S1_04_FACTORS_SCALING_PRIME_COILS.md">Factors, Scaling, And Prime Coils</a></p>
<p data-paper-ref="papers/phase2/PAPER_P2_05_PROOF_CARRYING_GLYPHS.md">Glyph paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/phase2/PAPER_P2_05_PROOF_CARRYING_GLYPHS.md">Proof-Carrying Glyphs</a></p>
<p data-paper-ref="papers/physics/PAPER_PHYS_01_PROOF_CARRYING_LATTICE_GAUGE.md">Physics source paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/physics/PAPER_PHYS_01_PROOF_CARRYING_LATTICE_GAUGE.md">Proof-Carrying Finite Lattice Gauge Links</a></p>

::: {.warning-box}
The Python fixture is an executable reference. The formal path is to encode exact finite regeneration facts in Lean and keep description-length comparisons separate from optimal-compression claims.
:::

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
